channel.title: LoopRails, Human-in-the-Loop & AI Agent Safety
channel.description: Practical, sourced writing on human-in-the-loop oversight of AI agents, when review helps, when it's a rubber stamp, and how to design oversight that actually catches mistakes.

item.title: What Is Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) in AI?
item.description: Human-in-the-loop (HITL) means a person reviews or can intervene in an AI system's actions. A practical guide to HITL for AI agents, what it is, when it works, and when to prevent instead.

item.title: Does Human-in-the-Loop Improve AI Safety?
item.description: Does keeping a human in the loop actually make AI agents safer? The evidence, when HITL helps, when it's false safety, and what real AI agent safety looks like.

item.title: In-the-Loop vs On-the-Loop vs Out-of-the-Loop
item.description: Human-in-the-loop, human-on-the-loop, and out-of-the-loop explained: definitions, tradeoffs, the sudden-handoff problem, and how to choose oversight for AI agents.

item.title: When Should an AI Agent Ask for Approval?
item.description: When AI agents should ask for human approval, and how to build approval gates that catch mistakes instead of becoming rubber stamps. Graded examples G0-G3.

item.title: The Lethal Trifecta: How AI Agents Leak Data
item.description: The lethal trifecta, private data + untrusted content + an exfiltration channel, lets prompt injection steal data from AI agents. How it works and how to stop it.

item.title: AI Agent Guardrails: A Practical Checklist
item.description: A practical AI agent guardrails checklist: sandboxing, least privilege, blast-radius caps, kill switches, circuit breakers, logging, and maker-checker, matched to risk.

item.title: AI Agent Autonomy Levels (L0-L6)
item.description: AI agent autonomy levels explained: the L0-L6 ladder from silent autonomy to escalate-or-forbid, and how to pick the right level for each action by risk.

item.title: Prompt Injection Prevention
item.description: How to prevent prompt injection in AI agents: why filtering fails, and a defense-in-depth approach, least privilege, runtime shields, sandboxing, and removing a lethal-trifecta leg.

item.title: Maker-Checker (Four-Eyes) for AI Agents
item.description: Maker-checker and the four-eyes principle for AI agents: why the proposer shouldn't be the approver, which actions need it, and how to implement it without rubber-stamping.

item.title: Automation Bias: Why People Rubber-Stamp AI
item.description: Automation bias is why human-in-the-loop oversight of AI fails: people over-trust the system and approve without scrutiny. The evidence, and how to design against it.

item.title: How to Build an AI Kill Switch
item.description: What an AI kill switch is, why every agent needs one, and how to design one that stops everything in flight, fast, reachable by anyone, and blame-free.

item.title: Study: How AI Agent Skills Leak Credentials
item.description: A 2026 study analyzed 17,022 AI agent skills and found rampant credential leaks, mostly via debug logging, during routine use. What it found and how to prevent it.

item.title: AI Agent Sandboxing
item.description: What AI agent sandboxing is and why it beats per-action approval prompts: no-network containers, scoped credentials, resource caps, and disposable environments.

item.title: Least Privilege for AI Agents
item.description: Least privilege for AI agents: give an agent only the tools, data, and credentials it needs, and why removing a capability beats forbidding its use.

item.title: The Circuit Breaker Pattern for AI Agents
item.description: A circuit breaker auto-pauses an AI agent when error rate, spend, or volume crosses a threshold, and requires human re-authorization to resume. How to build one.

item.title: What Is Agentic AI?
item.description: Agentic AI explained: how AI agents plan and take actions with tools, what makes them powerful and risky, and why overseeing them means governing actions, not outputs.

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop for AI Coding Agents
item.description: How to build human-in-the-loop oversight for AI coding agents: grade reads, edits, commits, merges, and shell actions G0-G3, and match the right control to each.

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop for AI Customer Support
item.description: How to build human-in-the-loop oversight for AI customer support agents: value-conditional approval for refunds, review for outbound replies, and escalation done right.

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop for AI Financial Transactions
item.description: How to build human-in-the-loop oversight for AI agents that move money: maker-checker, value thresholds, circuit breakers, and kill switches for irreversible payments.

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop for AI Database Operations
item.description: How to build human-in-the-loop oversight for AI agents that run SQL: read-only by default, dry-runs, least privilege, backups, and maker-checker for prod schema changes.

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop for AI Email & Messaging
item.description: How to build human-in-the-loop oversight for AI agents that send email and messages: undo-send windows, previews, rate caps, and approval for external or bulk sends.

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop for AI Deployments
item.description: How to build human-in-the-loop oversight for AI-driven deployments: canary plus automatic rollback, circuit breakers, and a kill switch instead of a rubber-stamp approval.

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop for AI Content Moderation
item.description: How to build human-in-the-loop oversight for AI content moderation: confidence-based routing, reversible removals, appeals as escalation, and avoiding reviewer fatigue.

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop for Machine Learning
item.description: Human-in-the-loop machine learning explained: labeling, active learning, low-confidence review, and RLHF, how to route human effort by uncertainty and keep label quality high.

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop for AI in Healthcare
item.description: How to design human-in-the-loop oversight for clinical AI: keep a licensed clinician in command, fight alert fatigue, and reserve autonomy for low-stakes actions.

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop for AI Legal Work
item.description: How to design human-in-the-loop oversight for AI legal and contract work: verify citations, attorney sign-off, maker-checker for execution, and treating documents as untrusted.

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop for AI Hiring
item.description: How to design human-in-the-loop oversight for AI hiring: keep a human deciding advance/reject, audit for bias, and never auto-reject candidates at scale.

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop for Browser & Computer-Use Agents
item.description: How to design human-in-the-loop oversight for browser and computer-use agents: sandboxing, breaking the lethal trifecta, spend caps, and prompt-injection defense.

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop for AI Voice Agents
item.description: How to design human-in-the-loop oversight for real-time AI voice agents: limit capabilities, verbal confirmation, and warm handoff to a human for high-stakes calls.

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop for Multi-Agent Systems
item.description: How to design human-in-the-loop oversight for multi-agent systems: least privilege per sub-agent, provenance logging, one kill switch, and clear human accountability.

item.title: What Is Loop Engineering?
item.description: Loop engineering means building a system that prompts an AI agent, checks its output, and decides the next step until a goal is met. The prompts-to-loops ladder, and why the verifier is the hard part.

item.title: How to Build Your First Agent Loop
item.description: A practical guide to building your first AI agent loop: goal and done-conditions, the verifier, memory in a file, writer and reviewer subagents, and guardrails on by default.

item.title: Loop Patterns for Engineering & Data Science
item.description: Reusable agent-loop recipes for software and data science: test-fixing, refactor, dependency-upgrade, data-cleaning, and experiment loops, each with a goal, a done-condition, and a verifier.

item.title: Evaluation-Driven Development
item.description: In an autonomous loop, an automated check, not your gut, decides whether each change improved things. How evaluation-driven development works and how to build a verifier you can trust.

item.title: Oversight for Autonomous Loops
item.description: Loop engineering moves oversight from per-step prompts to the goal, the verifier, and a few human checkpoints. How to grade a loop's actions, cap its blast radius, and stop it when it runs away.
